disclaimer: For educational and research purposes only. Not medical advice. Consult a qualified healthcare professional before starting any protocol.

peptides.name: BPC-157
peptides.description: Synthetic 15-amino-acid peptide derived from gastric protein. One of the most researched tissue-repair peptides with demonstrated anti-inflammatory and healing properties in preclinical models.

peptides.name: TB-500
peptides.description: Synthetic peptide fragment of thymosin beta-4. Promotes angiogenesis, accelerates wound healing, and supports tissue regeneration through enhanced cell motility and blood vessel formation.

peptides.name: Ipamorelin
peptides.description: Selective growth hormone secretagogue (GHRP). Stimulates GH release without significantly elevating cortisol or ACTH. Excellent specificity profile among GH secretagogues.

dosing.notes: Inject on empty stomach. Best synergy with natural nocturnal GH pulse.

storage.notes: Allow to reach room temp before opening

peptides.name: CJC-1295 No DAC
peptides.description: Growth hormone releasing hormone (GHRH) analog. Short half-life (~30 min), mimics natural pulsatile GH release. Often stacked with Ipamorelin for synergistic GH stimulation.

dosing.notes: Stack with Ipamorelin for amplified GH release. Inject on empty stomach.

peptides.name: CJC-1295 DAC
peptides.description: Long-acting GHRH analog. DAC attachment extends half-life to ~8 days. Weekly or twice-weekly dosing. Maintains elevated GH/IGF-1 levels continuously.

storage.notes: Long half-life - less frequent injections required

peptides.name: Sermorelin
peptides.description: First 29 amino acids of GHRH. FDA-approved form for pediatric GH deficiency. Stimulates natural GH release from pituitary. Most studied GHRH peptide with longest safety track record.

peptides.name: Tesamorelin
peptides.description: Synthetic analog of GHRH. FDA-approved for HIV-associated lipodystrophy. Strong evidence for visceral fat reduction and IGF-1 elevation.

storage.notes: FDA-approved - do not freeze lyophilized form

peptides.name: GHRP-2
peptides.description: Second-generation GHRP with strong GH release. More potent than GHRP-6 with less hunger stimulation. Elevates cortisol and prolactin at higher doses.

dosing.notes: Up to 3x daily dosing possible. Inject on empty stomach for best GH pulse.

peptides.name: GHRP-6
peptides.description: First-generation GHRP. Strong GH secretagogue with notable appetite stimulation (via ghrelin pathway). Good for bulking phases and GH optimization.

dosing.notes: Significant hunger stimulation. Plan meals accordingly.

peptides.name: IGF-1 LR3
peptides.description: Long-acting analog of IGF-1 with extended half-life (~20-30 hrs). Directly stimulates muscle growth, protein synthesis, and fat metabolism. Powerful anabolic compound.

dosing.notes: Short cycles recommended. Monitor blood glucose. Desensitization occurs with prolonged use.

reconstitution.notes: Use acetic acid for reconstitution, NOT BAC water

storage.notes: Very temperature sensitive. Most delicate peptide in this list.

peptides.name: MGF
peptides.description: Splice variant of IGF-1 expressed in response to muscle damage. Activates satellite cells for muscle repair and growth. Short half-life - best used post-exercise.

peptides.name: Epitalon
peptides.description: Synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) based on epithalamin from pineal gland. Activates telomerase, may extend telomere length. One of most studied anti-aging peptides.

dosing.notes: Long break periods between cycles (6-12 months). Annual or biannual protocol is common.

peptides.name: Thymosin Alpha-1
peptides.description: Naturally occurring peptide derived from thymosin fraction 5. FDA-approved in multiple countries as Zadaxin® for hepatitis and immunodeficiency. Potent immune modulator.

peptides.name: Semaglutide
peptides.description: GLP-1 receptor agonist with 94% homology to human GLP-1. Weekly injection. Approved as Ozempic® and Wegovy®. Potent weight loss and blood sugar regulation.

dosing.notes: Titrate slowly over months to minimize GI side effects. Maintain same day of week for injection.

peptides.name: Tirzepatide
peptides.description: Dual GLP-1 and GIP receptor agonist. Approved as Mounjaro® (T2D) and Zepbound® (obesity). Superior weight loss outcomes vs semaglutide in clinical trials.

dosing.notes: Escalate dose every 4 weeks. Maintain consistent weekly timing.

peptides.name: Retatrutide
peptides.description: Triple-receptor agonist (GLP-1, GIP, glucagon). Most potent weight loss peptide in trials. Phase 3 clinical trials showing ~24% body weight reduction.

dosing.notes: Escalate extremely slowly - 1mg/month increments. Most potent GLP agent.

peptides.name: AOD-9604
peptides.description: Fragment of HGH (amino acids 176-191) with fat-burning properties. No effect on blood sugar or growth. Originally developed by Monash University for obesity treatment.

peptides.name: PT-141
peptides.description: Melanocortin receptor agonist. FDA-approved as Vyleesi® for female sexual dysfunction. Also studied for male erectile dysfunction. Acts centrally on brain rather than vascular system.

dosing.notes: As-needed dosing. Not for daily use. Maximum 1x per 24 hours.

peptides.name: Melanotan II
peptides.description: Synthetic analog of alpha-MSH. Stimulates melanogenesis (tanning), libido, and erectile function via melanocortin receptors. Non-specific melanocortin agonist.

dosing.notes: Start very low to assess nausea. Use sunscreen still required. Monitor moles.

peptides.name: GHK-Cu
peptides.description: Naturally occurring copper complex in human plasma. Potent wound healer, collagen stimulator, and anti-aging compound. Used topically and systemically.

peptides.name: DSIP
peptides.description: Endogenous neuropeptide that modulates sleep. Reduces stress hormones, promotes delta (deep) sleep, and has analgesic properties. Studied for stress and sleep disorders.

peptides.name: Selank
peptides.description: Synthetic analogue of tuftsin. Anxiolytic and nootropic peptide. Modulates GABA and serotonin systems. Russian-developed peptide with strong anxiolytic properties without sedation.

peptides.name: Semax
peptides.description: Synthetic ACTH analog. Strong nootropic and neuroprotective effects. Increases BDNF and NGF. Used in Russia as approved drug for stroke and cognitive disorders.

peptides.name: PE-22-28
peptides.description: Spadin analog targeting TREK-1 potassium channel. Antidepressant mechanism distinct from SSRIs. Rapid onset mood improvement with potential for depression treatment.

peptides.name: NAD+
peptides.description: Essential coenzyme in energy metabolism. Declines with age. IV or SubQ supplementation replenishes cellular NAD+ for mitochondrial function, DNA repair, and sirtuin activation.

dosing.notes: SubQ can cause burning sensation. Inject very slowly. IV preferred for higher doses.

peptides.name: MOTS-C
peptides.fullName: MOTS-C (Mitochondrial ORF of the 12S rRNA Type-C)
peptides.description: Mitochondria-derived peptide. Regulates metabolic homeostasis, insulin sensitivity, and exercise capacity. Mimics exercise at the cellular level.

peptides.name: SS-31
peptides.description: Szeto-Schiller peptide that targets mitochondrial inner membrane. Improves mitochondrial efficiency, reduces ROS, and improves heart/kidney function in clinical trials.

peptides.name: AICAR
peptides.description: AMPK activator. Mimics exercise adaptation at cellular level. Improves endurance, insulin sensitivity, and fat oxidation. Called an 'exercise in a pill' compound.

peptides.name: SLU-PP-332
peptides.description: Estrogen-related receptor (ERR) agonist. Activates genes involved in mitochondrial biogenesis and oxidative metabolism. Exercise-mimicking compound in early research.

peptides.name: Gonadorelin
peptides.description: Synthetic GnRH (gonadotropin-releasing hormone). Stimulates LH and FSH release from pituitary. Used to maintain testicular function and fertility during TRT or post-cycle.

peptides.name: HCG
peptides.description: Mimics LH in the body. Stimulates testosterone production and maintains testicular size/function. Used during TRT and PCT. Measured in International Units (IU).

peptides.name: HGH 191AA
peptides.description: Synthetic 191-amino acid growth hormone identical to natural human GH. The 'gold standard' of GH therapy. Measured in IU. Comprehensive anabolic and metabolic effects.

dosing.notes: Inject morning for fat loss benefits. Evening for GH pulse synergy. Avoid eating 1hr before/after.

reconstitution.notes: Extremely gentle reconstitution - inject BAC water down vial wall slowly

peptides.name: LL-37
peptides.description: Human cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide. Broad-spectrum antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and wound healing properties. Key component of innate immune defense.

peptides.name: KPV
peptides.description: C-terminal tripeptide of alpha-MSH. Potent anti-inflammatory. Specifically targets gut inflammation. Excellent for inflammatory bowel conditions.

peptides.name: Glutathione
peptides.description: Master antioxidant tripeptide. Neutralizes free radicals, supports liver detoxification, and brightens skin. Injectable form has superior bioavailability vs oral.

peptides.name: 5-Amino-1MQ
peptides.description: NNMT inhibitor. Reactivates brown fat metabolism. Studied for obesity reversal without exercise. Stimulates NAD+ pathway and fat cell reprogramming.

peptides.name: FOXO4-DRI
peptides.description: Senolytic peptide that selectively induces apoptosis in senescent (zombie) cells. Clears cellular senescence, demonstrated tissue rejuvenation in mouse models.

dosing.notes: Short pulse cycles with very long breaks. Senolytic - clears cells, needs recovery time.

peptides.name: SNAP-8
peptides.description: SNAP-25 mimic that reduces muscle contraction. Topical anti-wrinkle peptide similar to Botox mechanism but milder. Reduces expression lines.

peptides.name: Cagrilintide
peptides.description: Long-acting amylin analog. Reduces appetite via brainstem pathways. In CagriSema trials (with semaglutide), showed ~24% weight reduction. Complementary to GLP-1 therapy.

peptides.name: Oxytocin
peptides.description: Neuropeptide known as the 'love hormone'. Promotes social bonding, reduces anxiety, lowers cortisol. Used for social anxiety, PTSD, and bonding disorders.

peptides.name: L-Carnitine
peptides.description: Amino acid derivative essential for fatty acid transport into mitochondria. Enhances fat oxidation, athletic performance, and recovery. Injectable form has superior absorption.

peptides.name: Kisspeptin
peptides.description: Hypothalamic neuropeptide that triggers GnRH release. Upstream regulator of the HPG axis. Used for fertility, testosterone support, and puberty disorders.

peptides.name: Survodutide
peptides.description: Dual GLP-1 and glucagon receptor agonist. In Phase 3 trials for obesity and NASH. Glucagon component enhances liver fat clearance beyond GLP-1 alone.

peptides.name: Mazdutide
peptides.description: GLP-1/glucagon receptor co-agonist from Innovent Biologics. Chinese-developed obesity treatment showing strong weight loss in Phase 3 trials. Similar profile to survodutide.
